trigger: LLM was asked to 'wire up the monitoring component so it actually runs'. Single patch added an import and a call. No Interface Impact Scan was performed. 10 tests failed.
symptom: 10 tests fail after a 1-line import + 1-line call wiring patch. Failures span 5 different test files with 5 different error types.
bad_code:
# The patch (correct intent, incomplete execution):
# Added in orchestrator.py:
from .event_monitor import EventMonitor   # new import
# ...
self.monitor = EventMonitor(self.config)   # new wiring line

# Downstream failures:
# 1. test_orchestrator.py: NameError (EventMonitor not mocked)
# 2. test_metrics.py: ValueError (Duplicated timeseries) — EventMonitor registers same metric
# 3. test_pipeline.py: AssertionError (row[2] checks severity, now tests region after column insert)
# 4. test_routing.py: TypeError (counter requires labels after SD3 change in EventMonitor)
# 5. test_e2e.py: AssertionError (queue_depth assertion fails — dedup in EventMonitor absorbed test stimuli)
good_code:
# Interface Impact Scan BEFORE the patch:
# 1. List changed symbols: EventMonitor (new), self.monitor (new)
# 2. grep test references: grep -rn 'EventMonitor\|self.monitor' tests/
# 3. Check for metric name conflicts: grep -rn 'Counter\|Gauge' src/event_monitor.py vs existing
# 4. Check for positional index tests: grep -rn 'row\[' tests/
# 5. Check for dedup/filter behavior: grep -rn 'dedup\|rate.limit\|filter' src/event_monitor.py
# Fix all 5 impact sites before committing
detection_command: git diff --cached | grep '^+.*import\|^+.*self\.' | while read line; do sym=$(echo $line | grep -o '[A-Z][a-zA-Z]*'); grep -rn "$sym" tests/ --include='*.py'; done
notes: This sample represents the 10-regression cascade documented in the systematic-debugging case study. All 10 regressions are instances of SS1-SS5 and SD1-SD3 — the wiring patch activated 5 independently-broken test assumptions simultaneously.
